invalid_review_attempts.reason: stdin full-diff invocation was terminated after prolonged 0-byte output and produced no review payload
stdin compact invocation was terminated after prolonged 0-byte output and produced no review payload
prompt-argument JSON-only invocation returned fenced JSON plus extra prose and pending-verification text, so it was not accepted as the pack review

parsed_review.summary: CP00-175 is a clean descriptor-only evidence/review/UI-readiness bridge over RP04.P08.M06.S04-RP04.P09.M07.S06 (150 units). Within the reviewed excerpt it is internally consistent and side-effect-free: CP175 pack binding (range, unit_count 150, upstream CP00-174, next CP00-176, next_subphase RP04.P09.M07.S07) agrees with validator evidence (covered_units 150, next_pack CP00-176); deliverable/phase/micro_phase/micro_title/bridge_section distributions each sum to 150 and cross-reconcile, with micro_title fully derivable from micro_phase counts and bridge-section ranges matching their unit_counts and the declared first/last unit ids. No real client data, runtime permission evaluation, audit append, Hermes/Claude execution, network, or UI/DOM rendering occurs (all such flags false; results re-frozen via freezeEvidenceReviewUiReadinessBridgeResult and CP175 no-write attestation). Leak guard correctly keeps internal_reference_fields and prohibited_outputs out of the customer_facing_readiness_summary, and internal_readiness_evidence carries only descriptor IDs and false booleans, not sensitive payloads. Only a P3 dead-variable cleanup is noted; review scope is limited to the supplied excerpt, which truncates the catalog builder return.

findings.title: Unused serialized variable customerFacingRows in catalog builder
findings.description: In createMasterDataEvidenceReviewUiReadinessBridgeCatalog the line const customerFacingRows = JSON.stringify(rows.map(row => row.customer_facing_readiness_summary)) is computed but never referenced in the returned object within the visible excerpt; the aggregate customer-surface guarantee is instead satisfied per-row via all_customer_surfaces_trimmed. It mirrors serializedCustomerFacingReadinessSummary in the sibling descriptor function (which IS consumed by prohibited_output_absent), suggesting an intended catalog-level prohibited-output scan that was left unwired. No functional or security impact: pack is descriptor-only, all runtime/permission/audit/Hermes/Claude/UI flags are false, and counts reconcile (deliverable, phase, micro_phase, micro_title, and bridge_section distributions each total 150; first/last unit and 150-unit count match CP175 binding and validator covered_units 150 next_pack CP00-176). Confirmation is bounded by the excerpt truncating mid-return of this builder.
findings.required_action: If confirmed unused once the truncated tail is read, delete customerFacingRows; otherwise consume it via an explicit catalog-level prohibited_output_absent aggregate check so the serialization has a purpose.
findings.post_review_disposition: rejected_false_positive_due_truncated_excerpt: createMasterDataEvidenceReviewUiReadinessBridgeCatalog uses customerFacingRows for catalog-level prohibited_output_absent at packages/master-data/src/service.js:3491-3493; no code change required and no unresolved blocker remains.
